channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Runnin' Utes stun #17 Kansas, 74-67
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Gabe Madsen scored 24 points and became Utah's career leader in made 3-pointers as the Utes beat No. 17 Kansas 74-67 on Saturday night. Madsen's fifth and final 3-pointer of the game with 1:58 to play gave him the Utah career record of 288 made 3s.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: BYU rolls past Kansas State, 80-65
item.description: PROVO, Utah (AP) - Richie Saunders scored 17 points and grabbed 14 rebounds on Saturday night to help BYU beat Kansas State 80-65 and snap the Wildcats' six-game win streak. Dallin Hall scored 16 points and Fousseyni Traore 11 for BYU (17-8, 8-6 Big 12).
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: No. 11 Michigan State beats Illinois 79-65; Izzo passes Knight in Big Ten coaching victories
item.description: CHAMPAIGN, Ill. (AP) — Jaxson Kohler had 23 points and 10 rebounds, Tre Holloman scored 14 points and No. 11 Michigan State rallied to beat Illinois 79-65 on Saturday night giving the Spartans' Tom Izzo the most conference wins in Big Ten history.
item.dc:creator: STEVE STEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mac McClung pulls off 1st NBA dunk contest three-peat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Mac McClung will leave it up to others to decide where he ranks among the best dunkers ever. When it comes to the dunk contest, there is no doubt.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bucks star Damian Lillard thrilled All-Star weekend featured NBA HBCU Classic in his native Oakland
item.description: For Oakland native Damian Lillard and Warriors star Stephen Curry, shining a light on Oakland's deep sports history and the legacies of so many Black athletes through the NBA HBCU Classic meant so much during All-Star Weekend.
item.dc:creator: Janie Mccauley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: D.C. Mayor Bowser open to using public money to help fund multibillion-dollar Commanders stadium at RFK site
item.description: Last year, Washington Commanders owner Josh Harris said 2030 was a "reasonable target" date for construction of a new stadium for the NFL franchise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The new NBA All-Star format is here, and Sunday night will show whether it works
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — This will be an actual All-Star matchup. On one side, there's a team with 86 combined All-Star selections, $2.7 billion in on-court earnings and where every single player on the team is either an NBA champion, an Olympic gold medalist, or both.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NHL exec hopes Canadians avoid 'unfortunate situation' of booing US national anthem ahead of rivalry game
item.description: Ahead of Saturday's U.S.-Canada matchup at the 4 Nations Face-Off, an NHL executive is hoping Canada's fans do not boo the U.S. national anthem again.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dodgers star Shohei Ohtani throws 1st BP of spring training, taking a step toward possibly pitching
item.description: GLENDALE, Ariz. (AP) — Los Angeles Dodgers superstar Shohei Ohtani threw his first bullpen of spring training on Saturday, taking a step toward possibly pitching for the first time since 2023. The reigning World Series champion and National League MVP has not pitched in the big leagues since Aug.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: George Kittle supports Swedish friend Filip Forsberg at the 4 Nations Face-Off in Montreal
item.description: San Francisco 49ers tight end George Kittle is among the fans in attendance for the Sweden-Finland game at the 4 Nations Face-Off.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Whyno, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NASCAR disqualifies Kligerman for too-low truck after flag-planting win at Daytona. He is appealing
item.description: Full-time television analyst and part-time NASCAR driver Parker Kligerman claimed the checkered flag for the Truck Series season opener and promptly planted it in the grass by the Daytona International Speedway logo.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Draymond Green on the state of the NBA: It's 'boring,' the Warriors star says
item.description: Draymond Green had just spoken for a few minutes Saturday morning about the state of the game, about how he believes it’s officiated differently than it once was, how stars don’t get the benefit of being stars like they did in the past.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Shiffrin OK with 5th place despite missing a worlds slalom medal for the first time
item.description: SAALBACH-HINTERGLEMM, Austria (AP) — All that talk about a record-breaking 16th medal. And extending her perfect run of six medals in six career slalom races at the world championships.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DAMPF, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Left-hander Cole Ragans and Royals agree to $13.25 million, 3-year contract
item.description: SUPRISE, Ariz. (AP) — All-Star left-hander Cole Ragans and the Kansas City Royals agreed Friday to a $13.25 million, three-year contract. Ragans gets a $250,000 signing bonus and salaries of $1 million this year, $4.5 million in 2026 and $7.5 million in 2027.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Davis Thompson leads by 1 over Scottie Scheffler with Rory McIlroy lurking at Torrey Pines
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Davis Thompson capped a roller coaster of a back nine at Torrey Pines with a birdie to take the lead Friday in the Genesis Invitational. Scottie Scheffler had an unusually wild day off the tee and was right on his heels.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shaprio mocked for Cowboys dig at Eagles' Super Bowl parade speech
item.description: Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro was mocked by critics on social media after a questionable line during his speech at the Eagles' Super Bowl victory parade Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Philadelphia police officers reenact Eagles' tush push ahead of Super Bowl parade
item.description: Philadelphia police officers pulled off their own version of the Eagles' infamous tush push ahead of the team's Super Bowl parade Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CA lawmakers introduce bill protecting girls from trans athletes after state refuses to follow Trump's order
item.description: California lawmakers introduced three bills aimed to combat trans inclusion after the state said it won't comply with the Trump administration on the issue.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CMU Swimming continues dominance at RMAC Championships
item.description: The Mesa Mavericks are gearing up for Nationals next month, putting their talent on full display at the RMAC Championships
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Utah Warriors kick off 2025 season next week
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - About to begin its eighth season in Major League Rugby, the Utah Warriors are out to improve upon last year's 5-11 record. "I think we do really well this year," said fullback Sione Mahe.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: A new professional soccer team might be coming to this Southern California city
item.description: A new stadium and entertainment district fit for a professional soccer team might be coming to Los Angeles County.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Study finds Bears fans most heartbroken in the NFL
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — In Chicago, sports and heartbreak go hand-in-hand. The Bears just went 5-12 when ahead of the season, they had playoff aspirations. The Bulls are fresh off back-to-back demolitions from the Detroit Pistons and embarking on a rebuild heading into the NBA all-star break.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Muni will be free for NBA-All Star, Lunar New Year weekend, SFMTA announces
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- With thousands of people expected to descend on San Francisco this weekend for the NBA All-Stars and Lunar New Year Parade, officials are urging people to leave their cars at home.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Measure for county to back ban on trans women in sports fails
item.description: An effort to get San Diego County to throw its support behind a bill in Congress that would ban transgender girls and women from competing in women's sports failed when it was brought forward at the Board of Supervisors on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Padres sign Kyle Hart, left-hander with standout season in Korea
item.description: The San Diego Padres have agreed to a one-year contract with left-hander Kyle Hart out of Korea, according to an announcement by Major League Baseball Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Jazz turn the tables on Lakers in 131-119 victory
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - What a difference 48 hours make. On Monday, the L.A. Lakers blew away the Utah Jazz in Luka Doncic' Laker debut. But Wednesday in Salt Lake City, it was all Jazz.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Holmoe will be a part of search for his replacement
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - After 20 years on the job, Tom Holmoe decided this past summer that this would be his last year as BYU's athletic director. "I made the decision in July of this year," Holmoe said at his retirement press conference Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Countdown to the 2025 Daytona 500: Drivers get ready for season opener
item.description: The biggest race in NASCAR is only a few days away, where 40 drivers will compete to win “The Great American Race.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Southwestern College Baseball Coach Jay Martel Hits 200 Career Wins
item.description: Southwestern College baseball coach Jay Martel swinging for the fences now has hit the 200 win total and counting.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: It's a busy week in Colorado Mesa Maverick country
item.description:
Men’s Basketball. Women’s Basketball. Men’s Wrestling. Baseball. Softball. Swimming. Men’s Lacrosse. Women’s Lacrosse.
All eight of those teams are in action this week.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Madsen scores 28, but Utes lose at Cincinnati
item.description: CINCINNATI (ABC4 Sports) - Despite 28 points from Gabe Madsen against his former team, the Runnin' Utes fell at Cincinnati Tuesday night, 85-75. Madsen, who began his college career with the Bearcats, made eight three-pointers on 22 attempts, in matching a season high point total.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Thoughts on the Chicago Bears from around the NFL on Radio Row during Super Bowl week
item.description: Chicago Bears new head coach Ben Johnson is expected to bring a fresh perspective to the team, while quarterback Caleb Williams is expected to elevate his game with the help of Johnson's expertise.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Member of Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show won't face charges for flag protest
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The cast member who displayed the Palestinian flag with the word “GAZA” in protest during Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show will not face charges, New Orleans police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Anthony Davis is out with groin strain for second game with Dallas Mavericks amid reports of longer absence
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Anthony Davis has been ruled out of what would have been his second game with the Mavericks amid reports that a groin injury sustained in his Dallas debut could sideline him for multiple weeks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Brewers unveil patch they will wear on their uniforms to honor Bob Uecker
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — The Milwaukee Brewers have unveiled a patch they will wear on their uniform this season to honor longtime broadcaster Bob Uecker, who died last month. The patch will appear on the sleeve of the Brewers’ uniforms.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Utah HC earns first shootout victory over Capitals
item.description: WASHINGTON D.C. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club is carrying a ton of momentum into the Four Nations Break. Karel Vejmelka made 30 saves in relief, and the Utah Hockey Club recovered for a 5-4 shootout win against the Washington Capitals at Capital One Arena on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Jets tell Aaron Rodgers they're 'moving on' from the QB for next season, report says
item.description: Aaron Rodgers is still contemplating whether to continue his playing career. If he does, it appears it won’t be with the New York Jets. The team has told the 41-year-old quarterback it is “moving on from him,” Fox Sports reported Sunday.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK Jr., Associated Press, WGN 9
